introSection: Bobbahn is a cancelled bobsled roller coaster that was to be installed at Phantasialand in Brühl, North Rhine-Westphalia, Germany.

sections.title: History
sections.content:
The ride originally appeared as an Intamin bobsled roller coaster on park maps, located where Colorado Adventure exists today. The ride was intertwined with the park's existing Log Flume, Wildwasserbahn. The coaster was to be named Bobbahn. The ride would be themed to the alpes, with a station based on the buildings from the region.

This ride was eventually cancelled due to the timing; both Efteling and Europa-Park were constructing bobsled coasters at the time, making it hard to actively compete with them, prompting the park to cancel the project altogether.

fullText:
Bobbahn is a cancelled bobsled roller coaster that was to be installed at Phantasialand in Brühl, North Rhine-Westphalia, Germany.

The ride originally appeared as an Intamin bobsled roller coaster on park maps, located where Colorado Adventure exists today. The ride was intertwined with the park's existing Log Flume, Wildwasserbahn. The coaster was to be named Bobbahn. The ride would be themed to the alpes, with a station based on the buildings from the region.

This ride was eventually cancelled due to the timing; both Efteling and Europa-Park were constructing bobsled coasters at the time, making it hard to actively compete with them, prompting the park to cancel the project altogether.
